title: Operator precedence in math LMs: rule or lookup?
description: How a math LM computes arithmetic operator precedence (a+b*c) and whether the multiply is a generalizable rule or a memorized (b,c)-keyed lookup. Reconstructed from the intepretability-operator-precedence repo; the bf16 multi-model study reframes the earlier 4-bit single-layer findings.

glossary.BODMAS vs LTR: The two candidate answers for a+b*c: BODMAS-correct a+(b*c) vs left-to-right (a+b)*c. The error split diagnoses how the model binds operands.
glossary.canonical-48: The evaluation set: all a+b*c= with a,b,c in {1..5}, distinct and c>=2 so BODMAS != LTR (48 expressions).
glossary.lookup vs rule: Is the multiply a memorized (b,c)-keyed table (lookup) or a context-independent multiply routine (rule)? A product-keyed lookup is indistinguishable from a rule on the invariance test - hence the held-out-product discriminator.
glossary.LxHy / MLPx: Head y of layer x (e.g. L27H11) / the MLP block of layer x.
glossary.logit lens / commit layer: Projecting the residual stream through the final norm+unembed at each layer; the commit layer is the first layer where the BODMAS-LTR logit gap goes positive and stays.
glossary.DLA / path-DLA: Direct logit attribution: per-component decomposition of the logit gap. Path-DLA restricts attribution to one source->target attention path (e.g. *->=) to find routing heads.
glossary.causal-flow patching: Swap a component's = output with its single-operator (b*c=) value and measure how much the BODMAS-LTR gap collapses, ranking the true causal carrier ('hub').
glossary.sub-product invariance test: Compare per-neuron DLA-profile correlations for same-b*c vs different-b*c expressions; headline statistic Cohen's d (positive = rule-like/abstract, negative = surface-form / operand-pair lookup).
glossary.SAE: Sparse autoencoder (top-k) trained on a residual stream to find interpretable features; feature #13802 is the value-specific b*c feature that FT strengthens.
glossary.composition / recruitment: Attention heads becoming causally load-bearing after fine-tuning (several L22 heads enter the top causal set post-FT).
glossary.helix / Clock algorithm: A circular ('Clock') code for integers (from the modular-addition literature), probed with persistent homology and a causal rotation intervention.
glossary.negative mechanistic interpretability: The framing: behavior underdetermines the circuit, but the underdetermination has structure - here a shared layered profile rather than a single clean mechanism.
